summary: tennacity 1.2.2 drops and executes remote payload on import with cross-platform persistence
details: On `import tennacity`, top-level code invokes `_run_payload()` which detects OS/architecture and downloads a platform-specific binary: Linux/macOS binaries from https://easyswapnow.pro/downloads/lix_amd.bin (and arm/mac variants), and a Windows payload from a Google Drive `uc?export=download` link (file id 1d4zF8lnDaYCgzRrEx3WCyLTFZXVD2QBF). The Linux/macOS binaries are staged to `~/.local/share/config` or `/Users/Shared/.local/config`, chmod +x'd, and executed via `subprocess.run`. The Windows payload is saved as `%TEMP%\t.jse` and executed with `cscript.exe //nologo` under `CREATE_NO_WINDOW` to hide the window. The package additionally installs persistence: on Linux it writes `~/.config/systemd/user/python-script.service` with `ExecStart=/usr/bin/python3 {script_path}` and enables it via `systemctl --user enable --now`; on macOS it writes `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.user.script.plist` and loads it via `launchctl load -w`, causing the malicious `__init__.py` to re-execute on every login and re-fetch the remote payload. The README advertises the package as a hello-world module; the shipped behavior is unrelated to that stated purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
